threats.title: Weak Authentication Mechanism
threats.description: The system lacks detailed information regarding its authentication mechanisms. Without knowing the specific authentication protocols (e.g., MFA, password complexity requirements, session management) and their implementation, attackers can potentially bypass authentication controls through brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication logic itself.  The absence of multi-factor authentication (MFA) significantly increases the risk.
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication logic, weak password policies, or lack of MFA.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all user accounts.
Enforce strong password policies (minimum length, complexity, regular rotation).
Regularly audit authentication logs for suspicious activity and implement intrusion detection systems (IDS) to monitor authentication attempts.

threats.title: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
threats.description: Without details on the system’s data handling processes, it’s highly likely that user-supplied input is not adequately validated or sanitized before being used in database queries or other operations. This creates an opportunity for attackers to inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection) or manipulate data, potentially leading to data corruption, unauthorized access, or system compromise.
threats.affected_component: All Data Processing Components (Web Application, API, Database)
threats.attack_vector: Injection of malicious SQL queries, JavaScript, or other code through vulnerable input fields.
mitigations: Implement robust input validation and sanitization techniques for all user-supplied data.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
threats.description: The lack of information about the system's architecture and resource constraints makes it difficult to assess the risk of denial-of-service attacks.  However, if the system is vulnerable to input-based attacks (as suggested by the data injection threat), an attacker could potentially flood the system with requests, consuming excessive resources and causing a denial of service.
threats.attack_vector: Flooding the system with malicious requests, exploiting vulnerabilities in the application logic.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.
Utilize a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious traffic.
Employ load balancing and auto-scaling to handle increased traffic volumes.

threats.title: IDOR Vulnerability
threats.description: Without knowing the system’s authorization mechanisms, it’s impossible to determine if there’s a risk of IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerabilities.  If users can manipulate identifiers (e.g., user IDs, file names) in URLs or API requests to access resources they shouldn’t have access to, attackers could potentially escalate their privileges or gain unauthorized access to sensitive data.
threats.attack_vector: Manipulating identifiers in requests to access unauthorized resources.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization checks for all API endpoints and web applications.
Use indirect object references (e.g., GUIDs) instead of direct identifiers.
Regularly audit access control logs for suspicious activity.

threats.title: Exposure of Sensitive Data due to Lack of Access Controls
threats.description: The absence of data classification details makes it impossible to determine the sensitivity of the data handled by the ‘csa’ system. Without knowing what data is considered confidential, it’s difficult to implement appropriate access controls. This could lead to unauthorized disclosure of sensitive information if users have excessive permissions or if vulnerabilities exist in the system’s access control mechanisms.
threats.attack_vector: Unauthorized access to data due to insufficient or misconfigured access controls.
mitigations: Implement a data classification scheme to categorize data based on sensitivity.
Apply the principle of least privilege, granting users only the minimum necessary access rights.
Regularly review and update access controls to ensure they remain appropriate.

gaps: Insufficient coverage of AC controls beyond the identified threats.
Limited implementation of IA controls related to authentication and access management.
Lack of comprehensive SI controls addressing broader information security aspects.
ASVS coverage is limited to specific vulnerability categories.
priorities: High: Weak Authentication Mechanism (AC-17, AU-2, AU-3, IA-2) - Critical severity, impacts all threats.
Medium: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input (AC-4, SC-28) - Critical severity, core vulnerability mitigation.

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Mechanism
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: IDOR Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Exposure of Sensitive Data due to Lack of Access Controls
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
